skills.name: Production Proposal Drafting
skills.description: Analyze one production script into a UI-reviewable production proposal draft.
skills.appliesWhen: 制作编排,剧集编排,制作编排,生产包,production proposal,production_proposal,orchestrate production,production package,拆分情节,素材需求,设定资料
skills.instruction: Production proposal is a preparation stage, not final execution. The agent has three contexts: 1) the current local production_proposal draft, which is the only write target; 2) the current real production, which is read-only; 3) project-level creative references and asset slots, which are read-only context for reuse and deduplication. The current production_proposal draft is a snapshot, not an operation log. Never directly create, update, or delete backend project entities for this workflow. The UI compares the production_proposal draft with the real production and asks the human to confirm before applying anything. Required execution chain: Step 1 read context: call movscript_get_current_context when available, call movscript_list_productions only if the production is missing or ambiguous, then call movscript_read_current_production for the selected production. Step 2 audit creative settings: decide whether project-level creative references satisfy this production; propose reuse/update/create only when needed and preserve confirmed entities. Step 3 audit asset needs: decide whether current asset slots satisfy each setting/state/scene moment; add only missing asset needs and mark priority/reason. Step 4 derive this production's segment rhythm and content storyboard: summarize emotional/rhythm/dramatic-function segments, each scene moment's expressed situation, and split each scene moment into content_units when multiple visual/narration/caption/transition/music beats are needed. Step 5 compare your derived segments, scene moments, content units, settings, and asset needs with current confirmed entities and the current local proposal draft; prefer keep/reuse, propose update only with a reason, and never delete confirmed entities. Use movscript_build_orchestration_diff before writing the final proposal whenever it is available; use its coverage and diff output to set action, id, rationale, coverage_status, diff_action, and source_evidence on proposal nodes. Then call movscript_preview_production_proposal_apply before finalizing, and use its semantic changes and warnings to validate that the draft can be written without unexpected backend side effects. This workflow may generate content_units and keyframe intent nodes as reviewable storyboard proposal: describe content type, visual intent, shot_size, camera_angle, duration, order, visual anchor, and prompt intent. Do not generate final media resources, final dialogue, or camera movement execution tables. Each production_proposal node should carry action create, reuse, or update. Project_proposal is different: it is a partial semantic merge patch over only creative_references and asset_slots. For project_proposal, do not write action, entity, target_id, source_ids, payload, or operations. Only include nodes and fields that should be merged; unmentioned existing project data remains unchanged. Use id to merge fields into an existing setting or asset need, omit id only for new candidates with fields.name, and express setting merges with merge_candidates on the retained creative_reference node. Project_proposal settings must have precise positioning, not vague mood labels: each new or revised creative_reference should make clear what it is, its kind, story/use function, observable visual or spatial traits, boundaries, and why it belongs to the current production/script. Each asset_slot should make clear the target object, view or deliverable, reusable use case, constraints, and acceptance basis. Do not use vague words such as premium, mysterious, atmospheric, youthful, tense, unique, cinematic, or cyberpunk as standalone settings; pair any style word with visible concrete details. If context is insufficient for precise positioning, ask the user or record the missing information in impact_notes instead of inventing generic descriptions. Asset-slot ownership must use owner { type: "creative_reference", id/client_id } or fields.creative_reference_id. Do not turn main view or side view needs into separate creative references.
skills.outputContract: After creating or modifying a production proposal draft, answer with the proposalRef/draftId, productionId, projectId when known, current draft status, counts by segment, scene moment, content unit, keyframe, creative material, and asset need, plus the main assumptions for scene moments, content storyboard, creative materials, and asset needs. State that it is a local review draft, not applied project data. End with the exact proposalRef/draftId that future edits should continue from.
